title: Digital Public Infrastructure
headings: DPI Architecture Principles make digitisation inclusive & scalable

title: Home
body: The Centre for Open Societal Systems (COSS) drives global societal transformation by facilitating countries to develop their Digital Public Infrastructure and crafting compelling change narratives for adoption of Digital Public Goods to create impact at scale. Our mission is to enable equitable, inclusive societal transformation by providing trusted technical guidance & co-creating digital solutions to advance the global adoption of Digital Public Goods (DPGs). We aim to improve the lives of 1 billion people globally by 2030 through adoption of Digital Public Goods (DPGs). Having played a key role in enabling the quantum leap of change that digital India has seen over the last decade, the COSS team has amassed a wealth of experience that can help other countries in creating their own transformation stories using digital tools. Explore how to engage with COSS to address your unique societal challenges. Partner with COSS to help countries design, implement and scale Digital Public Infras

title: Connected devices and weArables lab
headings: Principal Investigator: Dr. Kurian Polachan , Senior Member IEEE

title: Country Partners
headings:
"MOSIP has had 27 country engagements till date across Asia Pacific, Africa, Latin America, and the Caribbean” Our technical and country dissemination teams engage closely with governments to ensure that technology is implemented
          in a manner that addresses specific geographical and cultural needs. Country governments have complete agency and ownership over the customisation of their national ID system, the choice of
          local System Integrator, their Automated Biometric Identification System (ABIS) providers, the kind of biometric
          modality involved in their national ID system, and the providers of all those biometric devices and solutions.
Robust, Secure, Responsive, and Sustainable Systems
Building Trust by Protecting Privacy and User Rights
The Value of Identity for All

title: The MOSIP Project
headings:
provide legal identity for all,
                    including birth registration by 2030

At the heart of every MOSIP-based ID system lies a commitment to serving the unique needs of a country and its
          residents.
